title: ATP DNS Policy v1.0
description: JSON Schema for the Agent Trust Protocol (ATP) DNS Policy, parsed from a DNS TXT record at _aria-policy.<domain>. Analogous to a DMARC record, it defines the admission policy a receiving system enforces when AI agents present their credentials.

min.description: Minimum trust level required for agent admission. L0=Anchored, L1=Identified, L2=Certified, L3=Sovereign.

enforce.description: Enforcement mode. 'monitor' logs but admits all; 'warn' admits with warning headers; 'strict' rejects non-compliant agents.

items.description: Required scope pattern. Supports wildcards: 'commerce.*' matches any scope beginning with 'commerce.'.

req.description: Required scopes. Agent must carry all listed scopes (or matching wildcards) to be admitted.

items.description: Prohibited scope pattern. Agent carrying any listed scope is rejected or flagged.

deny.description: Prohibited scopes. Agent carrying any listed scope is rejected (strict) or flagged (monitor/warn).

items.description: Intent Declaration field name that must be present in the agent's intent payload.

intent.description: Required intent fields. The agent's Intent Declaration must include all listed fields.

depth.description: Maximum delegation chain depth. Agents with a delegation depth exceeding this value are rejected.

rate.description: Per-agent rate limit. Format: N/hr or N/min. E.g., '100/hr', '10/min'.

qualify.description: Conditional requirements. Format: condition>requirement. E.g., 'sector:healthcare>iso27001' means healthcare-sector agents must carry ISO 27001 attestation.

rua.description: Aggregate report URI. HTTPS endpoint where ATP sends periodic compliance reports, modeled on DMARC rua.

ttl.description: Policy cache TTL in seconds. How long agents should cache this policy before re-fetching.
